system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# Nexa Paraguay — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://nexaparaguay.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay
- **Docker service:** nexa_web (2 replicas)

## Pages
Home, Blog, Contacto, FAQ, Nosotros, Servicios (visas, residency, property, business), Privacidad, Términos

## Content
4 locales: content/es.json, en.json, de.json, nl.json

## Build & Deploy
npm run build && docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod . && docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml nexa

## Critical Patterns
Most international (4 locales). Relocation focus. Document-heavy. WhatsApp contact.

messages.content:
Okay, here's the full picture of **Nexa Paraguay**:

---

## What it is

Relocation consultancy for Paraguay. Multi-language (EN/ES/DE/NL), static Next.js site on Docker Swarm behind Traefik. Live at `nexaparaguay.com`.

## Stack

- **Next.js 16.2.4** + React 19, Tailwind CSS 4
- **Static files** — reads content from JSON, pages from JSON (no DB, no API)
- **Docker Compose** (standalone `output: "standalone"`), 2 replicas in Swarm
- **Traefik** via docker-compose labels
- **Security headers** + 1yr CDN cache on images

## Content

4 locales (es, en, de, nl), each ~2300 lines of JSON. 20+ pages defined as JSON files in `pages/`. Blog in `content/blog/posts.json`.

## Brand

Nexa Navy (#1B2A4A) + Champagne (#C9A96E). Tokens defined in `tokens.json`.

## Repo state

**`/root/nexa-paraguay`** — local clone, up-to-date with origin/main, one untracked file (`docs/whatsapp-ai-integration.md`). No uncommitted changes otherwise.

**`/root/sites/nexa-paraguay`** — also cloned, 1 commit behind (older copy).

**PAB integration** — also managed in `/root/paragu-ai-builder/sites/nexa-paraguay`.

## Key gaps (from `site.json`)

- Analytics GA4 ID is placeholder (`G-XXXXXXXXXXX`)
- HubSpot portal IDs are placeholders
- Site served via `paragu-ai.com/s/es/nexa-paraguay` and `nexaparaguay.com` (dual)
- WhatsApp number is `[REDACTED_PHONE]` (need to verify it's correct)

---

What do you want to do? Work on content? Design? Deploy? Something specific you want me to tackle?

Our Services
The section is now clean and focused, appearing as a professional list of solutions.

1. Residency & Legal Documents
The essential first steps for your life in Paraguay.

Permanent Residency: Professional management of your residency permit application.

Paraguayan ID (Cédula): Full assistance in obtaining your local identification card.

Apostille & Translation: We handle the certified translation and legal verification of all necessary foreign documents.

2. Banking & Tax Setup
Establishing your financial footprint in the country.

Bank Account Opening: Guidance and support to successfully open local bank accounts.

Tax Registration (RUC): Setup of your Paraguayan taxpayer ID for personal or business use.

3. Residential Real Estate
Finding the right place to call home, whether you are staying for a month or a lifetime.

Apartment & House Rentals: Help navigating the local market to find high-quality rentals.

Property Purchases: Expert assistance in finding and buying residential property.

Due Diligence: We verify titles and legal standing for any property you intend to rent or buy to ensure your investment is safe.
The Ideal Path to Residency
A streamlined collaboration between your home country and your new home in Paraguay.

1. Select Your Services
Choose the specific support you need. We create a personalized checklist of documents you need to gather in your home country, ensuring you don't waste time on unnecessary paperwork.

2. Parallel Processing
While you arrange your documents at home, we begin the work on the ground. We prepare all local filings and arrange for any required Paraguayan translations or legalizations so everything is ready for your arrival.

3. The Operational Day
With your documents in hand, you fly to Paraguay. We escort you through all in-person appointments—migration, police records, and identification—consolidating the entire legal process into one efficient day.

4. Cédula & Financial Activation
Once your Paraguayan ID (Cédula) is issued, we activate your financial infrastructure. We handle your RUC (Tax ID) registration and facilitate the opening of your local bank accounts.

5. Settlement & Lifestyle
With your legal and financial foundation in place, we help you settle in. This includes finding the perfect house or apartment to rent or buy and sourcing a reliable vehicle.
[7:41 PM, 5/5/2026] Kiki Weiss Hermana: cOur Story: Experience Turned into Expertise
Nexa Paraguay was founded with a single mission: to transform the relocation process from a bureaucratic hurdle into a welcoming experience. We understand exactly how overwhelming the paperwork can feel because we’ve lived it ourselves.

Our founder is married to a Dutch national and personally navigated the complexities of international relocation and documentation. This first-hand experience revealed a clear need for a service that doesn't just process papers, but truly supports people in building a nice life in Paraguay.

We believe your move should be more than just "getting a permit"—it should be the beginning of your best life here. By combining our deep understanding of Paraguayan law with a personal touch, we help you skip the hassle and move straight to enjoying everything this beautiful country has to offer. this are answers provided by the client document them and make an update plan for the content and any areas of work we need to focus on

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal, read_file

# Check current page JSONs for services, story, about, proceso
pages_to_check = ["programas.json", "sobre.json", "proceso.json", "home.json", "comparacion.json"]
for p in pages_to_check:
    r = read_file(f"/root/nexa-paraguay/pages/{p}")
    print(f"=== {p} ({r['file_size']} bytes) ===")
    print(r["content"][:800])
    print()

# Also check en.json for services/about sections
r = read_file("/root/nexa-paraguay/content/en.json", limit=300)
print("=== en.json (first 300 lines) ===")
print(r["content"])
"}

{"content": "   601|    "faq": {
   602|      "title": "Pricing and program questions",
   603|      "items": [
   604|        {
   605|          "q": "What does the price include?",
   606|          "a": "Fees, VAT, official taxes, bank coordination and integrated advisory."
   607|        },
   608|        {
   609|          "q": "What is NOT included?",
   610|          "a": "Flights, accommodation, apostilles in country of origin, personal expenses."
   611|        },
   612|        {
   613|          "q": "How is payment made?",
   614|          "a": "Upfront by wire transfer. No additional fees."
   615|        },
   616|        {
   617|          "q": "Can I switch programs after starting?",
   618|          "a": "Yes. If mid-process you decide to upgrade (e.g. from Base to Business) we roll the paid amount into the new program and invoice the delta. No double fees."
   619|        },
   620|        {
   621|          "q": "Do I need to be in Paraguay to start?",
   622|          "a": "No. We handle document pre-validation remotely. You only travel for the single operational day (migration + cédula + bank, all coordinated)."
   623|        },
   624|        {
   625|          "q": "How long does the whole process take end-to-end?",
   626|          "a": "8–12 weeks typical. 6 weeks document prep + 1 trip + 2–4 weeks for residency approval + cédula issuance."
   627|        },
   628|        {
   629|          "q": "What happens if my residency is rejected?",
   630|          "a": "Rejections are rare with proper document prep. If it happens for a reason within our scope (e.g. missed requirement) we re-present at our cost."
   631|        },
   632|        {
   633|          "q": "Is the 10% territorial tax guaranteed?",
   634|          "a": "The 10% IRE rate is current Paraguayan law (Ley 6380). We keep you updated on any reforms and structure your setup to stay compliant under the rules that apply at the time."
   635|        }
   636|      ]
   637|    },

function.arguments:
{"path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/client-questionnaire-responses.md", "content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Client Questionnaire Responses (Kiki Weiss, May 5 2026)

## Our Services (3 groups, 9 services total)

### 1. Residency & Legal Documents
*Essential first steps for your life in Paraguay.*

- **Permanent Residency**: Professional management of your residency permit application.
- **Paraguayan ID (Cédula)**: Full assistance in obtaining your local identification card.
- **Apostille & Translation**: We handle the certified translation and legal verification of all necessary foreign documents.

### 2. Banking & Tax Setup
*Establishing your financial footprint in the country.*

- **Bank Account Opening**: Guidance and support to successfully open local bank accounts.
- **Tax Registration (RUC)**: Setup of your Paraguayan taxpayer ID for personal or business use.

### 3. Residential Real Estate
*Finding the right place to call home, whether you are staying for a month or a lifetime.*

- **Apartment & House Rentals**: Help navigating the local market to find high-quality rentals.
- **Property Purchases**: Expert assistance in finding and buying residential property.
- **Due Diligence**: We verify titles and legal standing for any property you intend to rent or buy to ensure your investment is safe.

---

## The Ideal Path to Residency (5-step process)
*A streamlined collaboration between your home country and your new home in Paraguay.*

1. **Select Your Services** — Choose specific support. We create a personalized checklist of documents you need to gather in your home country, ensuring you don't waste time on unnecessary paperwork.

2. **Parallel Processing** — While you arrange documents at home, we begin work on the ground. We prepare all local filings and arrange for any required Paraguayan translations or legalizations so everything is ready for your arrival.

3. **The Operational Day** — With documents in hand, you fly to Paraguay. We escort you through all in-person appointments — migration, police records, and identification — consolidating the entire legal process into one efficient day.

4. **Cédula & Financial Activation** — Once your Paraguayan ID (Cédula) is issued, we activate your financial infrastructure. We handle your RUC (Tax ID) registration and facilitate the opening of your local bank accounts.

5. **Settlement & Lifestyle** — With legal and financial foundation in place, we help you settle in. This includes finding the perfect house or apartment to rent or buy and sourcing a reliable vehicle.

---

## Our Story
*Experience Turned into Expertise*

Nexa Paraguay was founded with a single mission: to transform the relocation process from a bureaucratic hurdle into a welcoming experience. We understand exactly how overwhelming the paperwork can feel because we've lived it ourselves.

Our founder is married to a Dutch national and personally navigated the complexities of international relocation and documentation. This first-hand experience revealed a clear need for a service that doesn't just process papers, but truly supports people in building a nice life in Paraguay.

We believe your move should be more than just "getting a permit" — it should be the beginning of your best life here. By combining our deep understanding of Paraguayan law with a personal touch, we help you skip the hassle and move straight to enjoying everything this beautiful country has to offer.
"}

messages.content:
I now have the full picture. Here's the analysis and update plan:

---

## Nexa Paraguay — Client Content Analysis & Update Plan

### What the client provided (Kiki Weiss, May 5)

**3 new/revised content blocks:**
1. **Our Services** — 3 groups, 9 specific services (not the current 4-tier package model)
2. **The Ideal Path to Residency** — 5-step process (different from current 5-step on site)
3. **Our Story** — Founder story (married to Dutch national, personal experience)

### Gap Analysis

| Area | Current Site | Client Wants | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Services** | 4 tied-packages (Base $2.9k, Business $4.4k, Investor $6.9k, Land) | 9 a-la-carte services in 3 groups: Residency/Legal, Banking/Tax, Real Estate | Add a "Services" section showing a-la-carte options alongside the packages. Or restructure entirely. |
| **Process** | 5 steps: Consult → Documents → Op Day → Entity/Banking → Delivery | 5 steps: Select Services → Parallel Processing → The Op Day → Cédula & Financial Activation → Settlement & Lifestyle | Rewrite the process content to match client's narrative. Steps 3-4 are similar but wording and emphasis differ. Step 5 is new (Settlement). |
| **About/Story** | Generic about-team page, no founder story | Personal founder story (married to Dutch national, "lived it myself") | Replace/expand about content. This is the main emotional hook. |
| **Apostille & Translation** | Not listed as a service | Listed as specific service #3 | New service offering to add |
| **Due Diligence** | Only mentioned for land purchase | Listed as separate service for any property (rent or buy) | Broader due diligence offering |
| **Settlement/Lifestyle** | Missing entirely | Step 5 includes housing + vehicle sourcing | New content section |
| **Vehicle sourcing** | Not mentioned | Part of settlement step | Minor addition |

### Priority Work Plan

1. **Our Story** — Replace `aboutPage.hero` and add founder narrative in all 4 locales (es/en/de/nl)
2. **Process rewrite** — Rewrite `processPage.process.steps` to match the 5-step narrative client provided
3. **Services section** — Add a "Services" section to homepage showing the 3 groups + 9 services, positioned above or alongside the packages
4. **Apostille & Translation** — Add as a service description in all 4 locales
5. **Due Diligence expansion** — Broaden wording to cover rent + buy
6. **Settlement step** — Add housing + vehicle sourcing to process content
7. **Translate to all 4 locales** — Current en.json has it all; es/de/nl need parallel updates

### Key structural question

The site currently sells **packages** ($2.9k Base → $6.9k Investor). The client described **a-la-carte services**. These can coexist — the packages are bundles of those services — but we need to decide the visual hierarchy. I'd recommend: services first (shows breadth), then packages (the value bundles).

Want me to start implementing? I'd begin with the **Our Story** content since it's the most distinct from what's on the site now.
implement eerything
Let me start by reading all locales to understand the full structure, then batch the updates.

4. Cédula & Financial Activation
5. Settlement & Lifestyle (NEW — includes housing + vehicle sourcing)

for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]:

        d = json.load(f)

    # Fix step 5 description to include vehicle sourcing
    vehicle_texts = {
        "en": "With your legal and financial foundation in place, we help you settle in. This includes finding the perfect house or apartment to rent or buy and sourcing a reliable vehicle.",
        "es": "Con tu base legal y financiera establecida, te ayudamos a instalarte. Esto incluye encontrar la casa o apartamento perfecto para alquilar o comprar, y conseguir un vehículo confiable.",
        "nl": "Met je juridische en financiële basis op orde, helpen we je settelen. Dit omvat het vinden van het perfecte huis of appartement om te huren of kopen, en het vinden van een betrouwbaar voertuig.",
        "de": "Mit Ihrer rechtlichen und finanziellen Grundlage helfen wir Ihnen, sich niederzulassen. Dies umfasst die Suche nach der perfekten Wohnung oder dem perfekten Haus zur Miete oder zum Kauf sowie die Beschaffung eines zuverlässigen Fahrzeugs."
    }
    
    d["processPage"]["process"]["steps"][4]["description"] = vehicle_texts[lang]
    d["home"]["process"]["steps"][4]["description"] = vehicle_texts[lang]
    
    with open(path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    
    print(f"{lang}: calendly URL set, vehicle sourcing added")

# Validate all
for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]:
    with open(f"/root/nexa-paraguay/content/{lang}.json") as f:
        json.load(f)
    print(f"{lang}: valid")
"}

1. **No "Our Services" page** — services section exists on homepage/programs but there's no dedicated `/servicios` page. The navigation doesn't have a Services link.

2. **Footer nav** still points to "Quality of life" -> `/calidad-de-vida` but that's not a primary service. Should add direct links to the 3 service groups (Residency & Legal, Banking & Tax, Real Estate).

3. **Stats numbers** (`+500 families`, `+10 years experience`, `98%`) — not confirmed by client. These are in the data but Kiki never mentioned them.

4. **Testimonials** — only 2 in `testimonials.json` but `home.testimonials` shows 3. The client gave no testimonial content so these remain generic.

5. **Homepage hero and tagline** — `"Your Paraguay move, handled end-to-end"` and `"Your new start in Paraguay, simple and calm"` — pre-existing, not from client responses. Should be aligned with the new "Experience Turned into Expertise" / services-first narrative.

Review the conversation above and update two things:

**Memory**: who the user is. Did the user reveal persona, desires, preferences, personal details, or expectations about how you should behave? Save facts about the user and durable preferences with the memory tool.

**Skills**: how to do this class of task. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the skill library: CLASS-LEVEL skills with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries.

Signals that warrant a skill update (any one is enough):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, verbosity, or approach. Frustration is a FIRST-CLASS skill signal, not just a memory signal. 'stop doing X', 'don't format like this', 'I hate when you Y' — embed the lesson in the skill that governs that task so the next session starts fixed.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, or debugging path emerged.
  • A skill that was loaded or consulted turned out wrong, missing, or outdated — patch it now.

Preference order for skills — pick the earliest that fits:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Check what skills were loaded via /skill-name or skill_view in the conversation. If one of them covers the learning, PATCH it first. It was in play; it's the right place.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (skills_list + skill_view to find the right one). Patch it.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella via skill_manage action=write_file. Three kinds: `references/<topic>.md` for session-specific detail OR condensed knowledge banks (quoted research, API docs excerpts, domain notes) written concise and task-focused; `templates/<name>.<ext>` for starter files meant to be copied and modified; `scripts/<name>.<ext>` for statically re-runnable actions (verification, fixture generators, probes). Add a one-line pointer in SKILL.md so future agents find them.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA when nothing exists. Name at the class level — NOT a PR number, error string, codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y' session artifact. If the name only fits today's task, fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding: when the user complains about how you handled a task, update the skill that governs that task — memory alone isn't enough. Memory says 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills say 'how to do this class of task for this user'. Both should carry user-preference lessons when relevant.

If you notice overlapping existing skills, mention it — the background curator handles consolidation.

Act on whichever of the two dimensions has real signal. If genuinely nothing stands out on either, say 'Nothing to save.' and stop — but don't reach for that conclusion as a default.

The `professional-services-website` skill is the closest existing umbrella — it covers standalone Next.js client websites. But it handles scaffolding/deployment, not content ingestion from client questionnaires. The `ecommerce-codebase-audit` skill covers content audit for e-commerce projects specifically.

This class of task — taking client questionnaire responses → gap analysis → implementing content changes across multiple locales and page configs in a Next.js site — is a recurring pattern for ParaguAI. Let me check if `professional-services-website` covers content updates:

# Professional-Services Website (Architecture A)

Build a standalone Next.js 15 site for a professional services firm
(consultancy, law firm, advisory) with dual-audience routing.

This is **Architecture A** — a per-client standalone repo.
For simpler single-page sites use the paragu-ai-builder instead.

## When to Use This vs the Builder

| Criteria | Use Standalone (Arch A) | Use Builder (Arch B) |
|---|---|---|
| Dual-path routing (investor vs business) | ✅ | ❌ (single flow) |
| Age-gate / path-choice modal | ✅ | ❌ |
| Complex comparison tables | ✅ | Limited |
| FAQ with multi-tab switching | ✅ | Limited |
| 5+ custom pages | ✅ | ❌ (builder does 1 page) |
| Team has 3+ staff profiles | ✅ | ✅ |
| Simple brochure (home + contact) | ❌ | ✅ |

## Project Structure

```
client-site/
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── layout.tsx          # providers + metadata + fonts
│   │   ├── page.tsx            # root page → routes to path
│   │   └── globals.css         # theme variables
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── EntryModal.tsx      # language + path choice modal
│   │   ├── InvestorLanding.tsx # full investor path page
│   │   ├── BusinessLanding.tsx # full business path page
│   │   ├── ComparisonTable.tsx # program comparison
│   │   ├── SuccessStory.tsx    # featured case study
│   │   └── BusinessFAQ.tsx     # 3-tab FAQ accordion
│   ├── content/
│   │   ├── data.json           # all copy, programs, languages
│   │   ├── types.ts            # TypeScript interfaces
│   │   └── index.ts            # getter functions
│   └── lib/
│       └── locale-context.tsx   # locale + path state management
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
├── next.config.ts
└── package.json
```

## Standard Workflow

### 1. Scaffold

```bash
npx create-next-app@latest <project-name> \
  --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir \
  --import-alias "@/*" --use-npm --no-turbopack
```

### 2. Content JSON

Single `src/content/data.json` with all localized text.
Structure per language:

```json
{
  "languages": [{"code": "en", "name": "English", "flag": "🇬🇧"}, ...],
  "programs": { "temporaryResidency": {...}, ... },
  "en": {
    "entry": { "title": "...", "pathResidency": "...", ... },
    "investor": { "hero": {...}, "team": {...}, "process": {...}, ... },
    "business": { "hero": {...}, "services": {...}, "faq": {...}, ... }
  },
  "es": { ... }
}
```

### 3. Entry Modal (Dual-Audience Gate)

- **Language selector**: dropdown (not grid). Shows flag + language name. Dropdown closes on click-outside. Checkmark on selected. No language descriptions.
- **Path buttons**: Two: investor / business. Each has short title + 1-line description (localized per language, for all 7 langs).
- `localStorage('gva-preferences')` persists choice
- Modal hidden after first selection
- Show spinner until `isReady` (hydration guard)

### 3b. Nav Language Dropdown

Replace the single back-to-gate button with a proper dropdown. Create a shared `LanguageDropdown.tsx`:

- Shows current flag + locale code + chevron (down when closed, up when open)
- Dropdown lists all languages from `content.languages` with flag + name + checkmark on active
- Click outside closes dropdown
- Calls `setLocale(l.code)` on selection — **switches language in-page** without going back to gate
- Position: `right-0 top-full` so it drops below the nav
- Import into both `InvestorLanding.tsx` and `BusinessLanding.tsx`

Keep **footer** back-to-gate button unchanged — it calls `reset()` which returns to the EntryModal. That's the escape hatch to change path.

Implementation: `setLocale()` is already exposed by the locale context. The dropdown only changes language, not path. Path changes still require `reset()` → gate.

### 4. Investor Path (single long landing page)

Dark-themed, gold-accented design. Sections in order:

1. **Sticky nav bar** (glass-panel, fixed top): brand name left, headliner links center, language (flag+code+arrow) + CTA button right. Headliner links: About, Process, Programs, Contact — small text, pill hover style.
2. **Hero**: full-screen, gradient overlay, large heading + subtitle + description + CTA button. Radial gold gradient background effect.
3. **About Us / Team**: section title + description + 3-column team card grid. Each card: initial avatar, name, role (gold text), description.
4. Track Record (4 stat counters)
5. Testimonials (2 columns)
6. Process (numbered steps, 5)
7. Comparison table (3 programs × 7 features)
8. Success story (featured case)
9. Final CTA
10. Footer with back-to-gate link

### 5. Business Path (multi-section page)

1. **Sticky nav bar**: brand left, headliner links (Industry, Services, FAQ, Contact) + language button + CTA right.
2. Hero
3. What Is Golden Visa (industry overview + stats)
4. Our Services (4 pillars, 2×2 grid)
5. Chain of Trust (timeline/flow diagram)
6. Investor Profiles (table: profile × needs × regions)
7. FAQ (3-tab accordion: Developers / Law Firms / Banks)
8. CTA
9. Footer with back-to-gate link

### 6. Deployment

```yaml
# docker-compose.yml — use agent-net (not traefik_public)
networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
```

```bash
docker build -t <name>:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml <name>
```

**Important:** The Swarm overlay network is `agent-net`, not `traefik_public`.
Check with `docker network ls | grep overlay`. All existing sites use `agent-net`.

### 7. Dockerfile (multi-stage, Alpine)

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine
# npm ci → build → standalone copy → run as nextjs user
# output: 'standalone' in next.config.ts
```

Set `output: 'standalone'` in next.config.ts for Docker builds.

## Content Management

All site copy lives in `src/content/data.json`:
- **English** is the default locale under `content.en.*`
- **Spanish** under `content.es.*`
- Add other locales by extending the object (PT, FR, IT, DE, NL)
- Program comparison data should match the same shape across locales
- Use `suffix` pattern: `field + 'Es'` or `field + 'Pt'` for localized variants

## Pitfalls

- **Spinner/loading state**: The EntryModal shows a spinner until `isReady` (localStorage hydration). This is expected. The modal will appear after hydration completes (~100ms). Don't remove the guard.
- **Network name**: Always use `agent-net` for Swarm stacks, not `traefik_public`. Verify with `docker network ls | grep overlay`.
- **Build output**: The Next.js `output: 'standalone'` config is required for Docker multi-stage builds. Without it, the runner stage won't have `server.js`.
- **Client-side routing only**: Both paths live on `/` with client-side routing via locale context. No additional Next.js routes needed.
- **Questionnaire**: Always produce a dual-language (ES/EN) questionnaire with 25-27 multiple-choice questions before building. This avoids rework when the client answers something unexpected.
- **Mobile nav**: Nav headliner links are `hidden md:flex` by default. No hamburger menu exists yet. On mobile, the only visible nav elements are the brand name, language dropdown, and CTA button. Must add a hamburger drawer for production.
- **Dead CTA links**: CTA buttons (`href="#contact*")` and WhatsApp buttons (`href="#"`) are placeholders. In production, WhatsApp links must use `https://wa.me/...` format. CTA buttons must link to a real Calendly/contact form or email. Check and fix before client review.
- **Business page silent failure**: If a locale's `business` section is missing fields (chainOfTrust, investorProfiles, stats) that the component renders via `.map()`, the entire page crashes silently with no error boundary. Each section must have either the full data or a null-conditional guard (`{data && (`). Test every locale by switching languages on the business path during QA.
- **Language content parity**: Every locale must have the same structure under its `business` section. The Spanish `es.business` is frequently missing `chainOfTrust`, `investorProfiles`, and `stats` — all of which the component renders. Missing fields cause blank page crashes with no error surface. When adding a new locale, copy the full `en.business` structure and translate.

## WhatsApp Integration

For clients needing a WhatsApp sales/support bot, see `references/whatsapp-sales-agent-deployment.md`. This pattern uses Evolution API + FastAPI + LightRAG for multi-client WhatsApp AI agents with business knowledge, product lookup, and conversation history.

Typical use cases:
- Real estate / visa advisors answering client questions automatically
- Lead qualification before human handoff
- After-hours FAQ automation

# Client Content Audit & Questionnaire Implementation

Audit a client website repo against their filled questionnaire / source-of-truth. Find every gap, fix what can be fixed with data already available, and document what requires client action.

## When to Use

- Client provided a questionnaire with answers and you need to verify implementation
- Taking over a partially-built client site and need to assess completeness
- Before client relaunch — ensure all content promises are delivered
- After a conversation with the client produced new data that needs to be incorporated
- **Pre-build questionnaire design** — client hasn't filled anything yet, you need to produce a structured intake form for a NEW client type not in the builder. Use the Block-style MC format (see references/questionnaire-ABCDE-pattern.md).

## Process

### 1. Ingest the Questionnaire

The questionnaire may be in `docs/` as `questionnaire.md`, `source-of-truth.md`, or `client-questionnaire.md`. Three tiers of detail:

- **`client-questionnaire.md`** — blank template (2KB, section headings only, no answers)
- **`questionnaire.md`** — filled form with client's answers (15-20KB)
- **`source-of-truth.md`** — structured canonical reference (10-15KB)

Read all three. The `source-of-truth.md` is the most authoritative — if it exists, use it as primary.

### 2. Map Questionnaire -> Content Inventory

Check every section of the questionnaire against the app's content:

```python
import json

# Read content
content = json.load(open("content/es.json"))
content_str = json.dumps(content).lower()

# Check each field
gaps = []
if "5618487" not in content_str:
    gaps.append("RUC not in content")
```

### 3. Categorize Gaps

Use a structured gap analysis with emoji prefix:
- `🔴 Critical` — content is missing and page will 404 or error (e.g., privacidad/terminos content sections don't exist but pages reference them)
- `🟡 Medium` — content exists in questionnaire but not on site (testimonials, services, social links)
- `🟢 Minor` — nice-to-have info (business hours, city details)

### 4. Fix What Can Be Fixed NOW

**Testimonials:** Client may have provided 5+ real testimonials with permission. Replace the placeholder/generic ones. Include: name, country, service received, quote, rating. DO NOT fabricate names — use verified client names only.

**Social Links:** The client's questionnaire almost always has Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, Wattpad, Amazon Author links that are nowhere on the site. Add them to the footer's `social` array and to the contact section.

**Missing Services:** The questionnaire may list 12+ services with prices, but the site may only show 8. Add the missing ones. Each needs: id, name, description, priceUSD, pricePYG, delivery time.

**Education/Credentials:** Client certifications (MIT, Google, etc.) and education rarely make it onto the about page. Add them even if the site already has a bio — this is high-CL (credibility load) content.

**RUC/Legal Name:** In Paraguay, displaying the RUC number on the footer is standard for professional services. Add it if missing.

**Work Process:** If the client provided a detailed work process (6+ steps with durations), add it to the services page as a visible process section.

**Blog:** If content has blog posts (`blog.posts`) but no `/blog` route exists, create the pages. Posts often have slug, title, excerpt, date, category, author, readingTime.

### 5. Sync to English (or secondary locale)

Most LATAM client sites are bilingual (ES/EN). After fixing ES content, sync the same changes to EN. Watch out for different key structures — ES may use `about.story.paragraphs` while EN uses `sobre.bio.features`. Check keys before blind-copying.

### 6. Generate/Save source-of-truth.md

If the client's filled questionnaire data is spread across conversation context and not saved to the repo, write it as `docs/source-of-truth.md`. This file becomes THE canonical reference for all future work. Structure it as:

```markdown
# Client Name — Source of Truth

> Complete client questionnaire answers. Canonical reference for all content.
> Source: Client onboarding session — Month Year

## PART A — CONFIRMATIONS

### A.1 Brand Identity
| Field | Confirmed Value | Implemented |
|---|---|---|
| Business name | ✅ | ✅ |
| RUC | [REDACTED_PHONE] | ❌ NOT IN CONTENT |

### A.3 Services with Prices ✅
| # | Service | USD | PYG | Delivery | Implemented |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Custom Cover eBook | $45 | ₲300,000 | 1-2 weeks | ✅ |
```

### 7. Client Drive Asset Ingestion

When the client shares a Google Drive folder with images/assets, DO NOT treat as "client action needed" — ingest them now. This is faster than waiting.

#### 7a. Download the Folder

```bash
# Install gdown if not present
pip install gdown

# Download entire folder by link
python3 -m gdown --folder "https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/<FOLDER_ID>" -O /tmp/<project-assets>

# Or for a single file by share link
python3 -m gdown "https://drive.google.com/uc?id=<FILE_ID>" -O /tmp/<output.ext>
```

#### 7b. Analyze Without Vision

When vision APIs are down, classify images by **metadata + naming patterns**:

```python
from PIL import Image
import os

path = '/tmp/assets'
files = sorted([f for f in os.listdir(path) if f.endswith(('.jpg','.png','.jfif','.webp'))])

for f in files:
    size_kb = os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f}') // 1024
    img = Image.open(f'{path}/{f}')
    w, h = img.size
    ratio = w/h
    
    # Classify by aspect ratio
    if ratio > 1.4:    shape = "WIDE — banner/badge row"
    elif ratio > 1.0:  shape = "LANDSCAPE — card/certificate"
    elif ratio > 0.85: shape = "SQUARISH — profile/badge"
    elif ratio > 0.7:  shape = "PORTRAIT — ranking/vertical"
    else:              shape = "TALL — individual item"
    
    # Classify by dimensions
    if w == 1080 and h == 2400:
        cat = "PHONE SCREENSHOT (Amazon KDP/Instagram analytics)"
    elif w == 1080 and h == 1350:
        cat = "INSTAGRAM POST"
    elif '_n.jpg' in f:
        cat = "INSTAGRAM-SOURCED"
    elif '-' in f and len(f.split('-')[0]) == 8:
        cat = f"UUID CROP ({shape})"
    else:
        cat = f"UNKNOWN ({shape})"
    
    print(f"  {f:<55} {size_kb:>4}KB  {cat}")
```

**Key heuristics for Drive folder names that reveal content type:**
- `Logros` = Achievement screenshots (KDP rankings, Prime badges, reader counts, certifications)
- `Logo editoriales` = Publisher/editorial logos for client's partner section
- `Fotos`, `Imágenes`, `Assets` = General brand imagery
- Naming with underscore + number (`_n.jpg`) = Instagram downloads
- UUID filenames = Exported/cropped from an app or screenshot tool

#### 7c. Convert Problematic Formats

```python
# .jfif files won't render in browsers — convert to .png
from PIL import Image
for f in os.listdir(path):
    if f.endswith('.jfif'):
        img = Image.open(f'{path}/{f}')
        out = f.replace('.jfif', '.png')
        img.save(f'{path}/{out}', 'PNG')
        # Keep original too in case client wants it
```

#### 7d. Organize Into Repo

Place files in `public/<project>/<category>/`:
- `public/dayah/logos/` — publisher logos, brand marks
- `public/dayah/achievements/` — badges, rankings, certificates (new section)
- `public/dayah/social/` — Instagram/marketing posts (existing)

Update site components to reference the new asset paths (inline image map or content file).

### 8. What to Leave for Client Action

Some things cannot be fixed by the agent and must be surfaced as "Client Action Still Needed":
- **Missing real photos** (premade catalog, professional photography) — client still owes these
- **Final brand palette** (exact hex codes, typography) — pending client design decisions
- **Mercado Pago/PayPal confirmation** — client must verify account
- **Domain registration** — client must purchase and point DNS
- **Drive folders without clear naming** — ask client what the files are

## Corrections Document Implementation (Post-Audit)

After the initial audit, clients often send a corrections/updates document (structured MD file) with immediate action items. This is distinct from the initial gap analysis — it's an authoritative list of changes from the client themselves.

### When to Expect This

- Client reviews the live/preview site and sends a document titled "Correcciones", "Cambios", "Final edits", or similar
- Usually a .md file with checkboxes, ✅ / ❌ / ⬜ markers
- Often arrives close to launch date (1-2 weeks before)
- Client may have appended it to the conversation as a text document

### Signal Detection

If a user sends a structured document with sections like "IMPLEMENTAR YA", "ACTUALIZACIONES INMEDIATAS", or a checklist, it's a corrections doc. Do NOT treat it as conversation context alone — save it to document cache and work from it systematically.

### Implementation Process

#### 1. Parse the document structure

Corrections docs typically have these section types:
- **✅ Inmediatas** — things to remove/change NOW (book removals, testimonial replacements, payment method changes)
- **⚠️ Pending client** — marked with ⬜, client will send later (photos, bios, portfolio images)
- **❌ Rejections** — features client doesn't want (music, credits, payment integrations)
- **✅ Approvals** — things client CONFIRMS (color palette, logos, fonts, domain)

#### 2. Batch operations, one-by-one changes

```python
# Batch all JSON edits in one script execution
import json

with open('content/es.json') as f:
    es = json.load(f)

# Remove items by title
es['home']['books']['items'] = [b for b in es['home']['books']['items'] if b['title'] != 'La Efímera Eternidad del Amor']

# Replace arrays entirely
es['home']['testimonials'] = [new_testimonial_objects]

# Update URLs in bulk
book_links = {'Intense': 'https://rxe.me/7V2YVQ', ...}
for book in es['home']['books']['items']:
    if book['title'] in book_links:
        book['platformUrl'] = book_links[book['title']]

# Remove payment provider strings across all sections (FAQ, terms, privacy, footer)
for section in es['terminos']['sections']:
    if 'Mercado Pago' in section['a']:
        section['a'] = section['a'].replace('Mercado Pago, ', '')

with open('content/es.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(es, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
```

#### 3. Update CSS/design tokens from corrections

Corrections often include an approved color palette:
- Update `app/globals.css` `@theme` block
- Update `meta[name="theme-color"]` in layout.tsx
- Update `content/tokens.json` if used

#### 4. Fix pre-existing build errors discovered during rebuild

Corrections session inevitably involves `npm run build` to verify. Pre-existing errors (not caused by your changes) WILL surface. Fix them rather than treating them as blockers:

- **TypeScript index signature errors**: `content.blogPost?.[slug as string]` fails because JSON-inferred types lack index signatures. Fix: `(content.blogPost as Record<string, any>)?.[slug as string]`
- **Missing required props**: Components that changed signature after you started may lack props. Listen to the build error — it tells you the exact missing prop and component.
- **WhatsAppFloat phone prop**: This component requires `phone` prop. If any page was missing it, the build will fail on that specific page.

#### 5. Sync changes to secondary locale

Most changes (payment removal, palette, testimonials) apply to EN locale too. But only fix the keys that exist — don't add sections that don't exist in the secondary locale.

#### 6. When to save a reference file

If the corrections document is long (100+ lines) and contains detailed client instructions that future sessions may need (like exact book links, testimonial full text, color hexes), save it as a reference file under the skill:

```bash
skill_manage(action='write_file', name='client-content-audit', 
  file_path='references/dayah-corrections-[REDACTED_PHONE].md',
  file_content='<document content>')
```

### Common Correction Patterns

| Pattern | Action |
|---|---|
| "Remove X from catalog" | Filter items array in JSON, check ALL locales |
| "Use ONLY these testimonials" | Replace entire testimonials array |
| "Eliminar música" | Remove audio elements, delete audio-related imports |
| "No credit to X" | Remove name/credits from about page, docs |
| "Paleta aprobada" | Update CSS theme block and meta theme-color |
| "Payment method X not available" | Remove from FAQ, terms, privacy, footer (all locales) |
| "Photo provisional" | Use existing photo, note it's temporary |
| "About section pending" | Keep current text, don't publish (if possible, add progress note) |

### What NOT to do

- Don't ask "which one" — the document is client-authoritative, just implement
- Don't re-audit — the client has already reviewed the site, trust their list
- Don't create new features unless the doc explicitly asks for them
- Don't renegotiate payment methods — if client says "MP not available in Paraguay", update immediately
- Don't ask for testimonials again — client already provided the authorized set
- Don't spend time on ⬜ items — those are client-pending, surface them in summary

## Multi-Locale Content Implementation (LATAM Relocation Site Pattern)

When a client sends free-form text (WhatsApp message, questionnaire) with structured content that needs to go into a multi-locale JSON-driven site:

### 1. Save Questionnaire Response First

Write the raw client response to `docs/client-questionnaire-responses.md` before touching any code. This is the source of truth.

### 2. Gap Analysis Structure

Produce a structured matrix before implementing:
| Area | Current Site | Client Wants | Action |
|---|---|---|---|

### 3. Batch JSON Manipulation (Critical)

**Do NOT use patch tool for large JSON content sections.** Patch is fragile with JSON — it can orphan sibling keys or leave stale content when fuzzy-matching multi-line blocks.

Instead:
- **Prefer direct JSON manipulation via python** — read with `json.load()`, modify dicts/lists, write with `json.dump(..., indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)`
- If you must use patch, target SMALL blocks (single key-value pair) with a unique anchor line
- Always validate after patch: `python3 -c 'import json; json.load(open(path))'`

### 4. Always Update ALL Locales

For LATAM relocation sites (en/es/nl/de), every content change must be replicated to all 4 locale files in the same session. The user will NOT ask you to do this — it's assumed.

Use a single Python script to batch-process all locale files:
```python
LANGUAGES = {"es": {...translations...}, "nl": {...}, "de": {...}, "en": {...}}
for lang, translations in LANGUAGES.items():
    data = json.load(open(f"content/{lang}.json"))
    data["home"]["services"] = translations["services"]
    ...
    json.dump(data, open(f"content/{lang}.json", "w"), indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
```

### 5. Update Page Configs Too

Content JSON changes (adding a new section like `home.services`) require corresponding page config updates:
- `pages/home.json` — add the section in the `sections` array
- `pages/programas.json` — if services should appear on programs page too
- `pages/sobre.json` — if adding story/narrative section

### 6. Verify and Commit

```bash
# Validate all files
for f in pages/*.json content/*.json; do python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('$f'))" && echo "$f: VALID"; done

git add -A && git commit -m "content: ..."
git push origin main
```

## Pitfalls

- **Patch can corrupt nested JSON** — If old_string matches in multiple locations or partial matches, patch may orphan sibling content. Always validate JSON after every patch operation. If complex, use Python JSON manipulation instead.
- **Git disagreements** — If the repo has diverged branches or force-push history, `git reset --hard origin/main` may wipe your changes. Always `git pull --rebase` first, or `git stash` before resetting.
- **EN vs ES key structures differ** — Never assume keys match. `content_es` might have `about` while `content_en` has `sobre`. Check both before syncing.
- **Drive folders with UUID filenames** — When a client shares a Drive folder with 40 files all named like UUIDs, download first, then classify by metadata (dimensions, format, naming). Don't assume you can use vision API — it may be down. Dimension-based classification + aspect ratio heuristics work well as fallback.
- **Full-height phone screenshots (1080×2400)** — These are almost always scroll-capture from a phone (KDP dashboard, Instagram profile). They WILL NOT fit in standard website sections. Either crop to the relevant portion or link to them as expandable proof elements rather than inline images.
- **.jfif files don't render in browsers** — Always convert to .png using PIL before committing.
- **Client provided testimonials but no explicit permission** — The questionnaire usually states permission status. Trust data from filled questionnaires. If unsure, use "Verified Client" instead of fabricated names.

## Content Pattern: Relocation Services Site

Client provided 3 content blocks via WhatsApp/chat:
1. **Our Services** — 3 groups, 9 a-la-carte services
2. **The Ideal Path to Residency** — 5-step process
3. **Our Story** — Founder personal narrative
